title: Ervatamia pandacaqui
taxonomy: Ervatamia pandacaqui is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the Philippines. This plant is classified under the genus Ervatamia, which is part of the family Apocynaceae. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The classification of Ervatamia pandacaqui reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family.
uses: Ervatamia pandacaqui is a plant species with reported medicinal uses. It is traditionally used in the Philippines to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. The plant's root and stem are used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as diarrhea and skin infections. The leaves and flowers are also used to treat fever and cough. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Ervatamia pandacaqui as a medicinal plant.
